title: equisetum arvense var. nemorosum
intro: Equisetum arvense var. nemorosum, also known as marsh horsetail, is a subspecies of the horsetail plant, belonging to the Equisetaceae family. This perennial plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high silica content and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are known to promote wound healing, reduce pain and swelling, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of marsh horsetail include its ability to support urinary tract health, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of marsh horsetail can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
